name: phptools-vscode
description: PHP IntelliSense, Debug (Xdebug), Code Actions, Linting, Refactoring, Formatter, PHPUnit tests debugging, Server, and more.

php.format.autoimport.markdownDescription: Completion of types, functions, and constants outside of the current namespace scope.
markdownEnumDescriptions: Fully qualifies the name upon completion.
Inserts the name as it is, without importing the alias.
Do not show inaccessible symbols in code completion.

php.format.autoimport-docblock.markdownDescription: Completion of type names outside of the current namespace scope, when completing inside a PHP doc block (e.g. in `@param` tag).
markdownEnumDescriptions: Fully qualifies the name upon completion.
Inserts the name as it is, without importing the alias.
Do not show inaccessible types in code completion.

php.linkedEditing.variables.markdownDescription: When `"editor.linkedEditing"` setting is enabled, this option specifies whether to apply linked edits to local variable names.

php.workspace.includePath.description: Paths containing additional PHP declarations. Separated with semi-colon.

php.executables.markdownDescription:
Defines PHP profiles as a user defined name and a `php` binary path.
Example: `"php.executables": { "v7.4": "/usr/bin/php7.4" }`

php.version.markdownDescription: A PHP version or a profile name defined in `"php.executables"`.

php.codeLens.enabled.description: Enable/disable code lenses in PHP files.

php.problems.scope.description: Which workspace folders gets analysed. Default is "user" which excludes "vendor" folder.
enumDescriptions: Workspace will be analysed excluding "vendor" folder.

php.problems.exclude.description: Path patterns to be ignored from reporting problems. Value can be set to `true` to ignore all problems or a comma separated list of specific problem codes.

php.problems.workspaceAnalysis.description: Deprecated. Enable full workspace-wide analysis. Enabled by default.

php.stubs.markdownDescription:
Array of extension names to be included in the built-in IntelliSense, the localized manual, and code analysis. By default all the standard PHP extensions are included.

Examples:

```json
{
  "php.stubs": ["*", "com"]
}
```

List of possible values:

- `"*"` includes the standard set of extensions.
- `"all"` includes all the available extensions.
- `com`
- `zip`
- `zlib`
- ...

php.debug.port.description: Default Xdebug port or array of ports. This value is used when `port` is not specified in `launch.json` settings. Usually 9000 or 9003.

phpTools.license.deprecationMessage: This is no longer used. You may delete this setting.

phpunit.phpunit.description: Path to PHPUnit binary to be used to run and debug the tests. It can be either full path or path relative to the workspace root.

commands.title: PHP: Start Debugging and Stop on Entry
Launch current script in console and debug (PHP)

externalConsole.description: Launch debug target in external console.

args.description: Command line arguments passed to the program.

cwd.description: Absolute path to the working directory of the program being debugged. Default is the current workspace.

phpVersion.description: PHP version or profile name defined in 'php.executables'

runtimeExecutable.description: Absolute path to the runtime executable to be used. Default is the runtime executable on the PATH.

runtimeArgs.description: Optional arguments passed to the runtime executable.

env.description: Environment variables passed to the program.

port.description: Port on which to listen for Xdebug

serverSourceRoot.description: Deprecated: The source root when debugging a remote host
serverSourceRoot.deprecationMessage: Property serverSourceRoot is deprecated, please use pathMappings to define a server root.

localSourceRoot.description: Deprecated: The source root on this machine that is the equivalent to the serverSourceRoot on the server.
localSourceRoot.deprecationMessage: Property localSourceRoot is deprecated, please use pathMappings to define a local root.

pathMappings.description: A mapping of server paths to local paths.

exclude.description: Glob patterns that the debugger won't debug.

log.description: If true, will log all communication between VS Code and the adapter

enable.description: If True, will connect to DBGp proxy configured in this section

host.description: The host address where the proxy is located. It's usually same as web server, but it's doesn't have to be.

port.description: The port where the proxy server is listening.

ideKey.description: The Xdebug IDE Key used to register the VS CODE. If not set, the current user name is used instead.

configurationSnippets.label: PHP: Launch built-in server
configurationSnippets.description: Launches PHP's built-in development server and starts debugging.

configurationSnippets.label: PHP: Launch current script in console
configurationSnippets.description: Launches currently opened PHP document as a console application.

configurationSnippets.label: PHP: Listen for Xdebug
configurationSnippets.description: Starts debugging by waiting for an incoming Xdebug connection from a remote server.

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with PHP Tools
walkthroughs.description: How to use PHP Tools, and what they do?

steps.title: Open a Folder or a Workspace
steps.description:
First **Open a Folder** with a ``.php`` file, so you can take advantage of advanced IntelliSense, configurable debugger, tests explorer, or to fine-tune your workspace settings.

[Open Folder](command:vscode.openFolder)

steps.title: Discover IntelliSense.
steps.description:
There is many features you would like to try.

Please see [the documentation](https://docs.devsense.com/en/vscode/editor/completion?from=vscode) for more information.

steps.title: Debug PHP Code
steps.description:
Install [Xdebug](https://xdebug.org/) and take advantage of Visual Studio Code debugging.

 - **``F5``** to start debugging.
 - **``F9``** to put a breakpoint.

See more at [the documentation page](https://docs.devsense.com/en/vscode/debug?from=vscode).

steps.title: Register your copy of PHP Tools
steps.description:
Get the most of the extension and advanced features by registering **PHP Tools**. 

  **[Features](https://www.devsense.com/features#vscode)** | **[Purchase](https://www.devsense.com/purchase?from=vscode) | **[Documentation](https://docs.devsense.com/vscode?from=vscode)**

[Activate](command:phptools.activate)

walkthroughs.title: Get started with PHP debugging
walkthroughs.description: How to install PHP and Xdebug and debug a PHP in VS Code.

steps.title: Install PHP and Xdebug
steps.description:
To debug you will need to install PHP and Xdebug. You can do it through the terminal.
[Open Terminal](command:workbench.action.terminal.new)

steps.title: Install PHP
steps.description:
To debug you will need to install PHP and Xdebug. You can do it through the terminal.
[Open Terminal](command:workbench.action.terminal.new)

steps.title: Install PHP
steps.description:
To debug you will need to install PHP and Xdebug. You can do it through the terminal.
[Open Terminal](command:workbench.action.terminal.new)

steps.title: Create a PHP file
steps.description:
[Open](command:toSide:workbench.action.files.openFile) or [create](command:toSide:workbench.action.files.newUntitledFile?%7B%22languageId%22%3A%22php%22%7D) a PHP file - make sure to save it as ".php".
[Create PHP File](command:toSide:workbench.action.files.newUntitledFile?%7B%22languageId%22%3A%22php%22%7D)

media.altText: Open a PHP file or a folder with a PHP project.

steps.title: Run and debug your PHP file
steps.description:
Open a PHP file, set a breakpoint using F9 and then press F5. Choose ``Launch current script in console``. 
[Learn more](https://docs.devsense.com/vscode/debug#launching-debug-in-vscode?from=vscode)

media.altText: How to run and debug in VS Code with F5

steps.title: Learn more
steps.description:
There are many more ways how to use the PHP debugger.
 You can start by exploring [Launch Profiles](https://docs.devsense.com/en/vscode/debug/launch-json?from=vscode)

virtualWorkspaces.description: In virtual workspaces, not all files might be loaded and resolving and finding references might be limited.

badges.description: PHP Tools for VS Code Changelog

scripts.compile: dotnet publish ../Devsense.PHP.LanguageServer --ignore-failed-sources --output out/server & npm run -S esbuild-base -- --sourcemap
